title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant threat to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing millions in delays and affecting multiple launch providers. The issue highlights safety and operational risks in the space industry.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
summary: Vantor, formerly Maxar Intelligence, launched WorldView 3D on July 1 to provide customers with high-definition and up-to-date imagery. The new offering aims to enhance data currency for various applications.

title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
summary: EchoStar's subsidiaries related to satellite TV and its abandoned 5G network have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. The move follows a prepackaged restructuring plan to repay debt early after selling spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T.

title: Latitude plans to conduct first launch from Oman
summary: French startup Latitude plans to conduct its first launch of a small launch vehicle from a spaceport in Oman in late 2027. This marks a significant step for the company's expansion into international launch operations.

title: Blue Origin outlines new launch pad approach as it pushes to return New Glenn to flight
summary: Blue Origin plans to return its New Glenn rocket to flight by the end of the year after a pad explosion, with a rebuilt launch pad approach. The company outlined its new strategy a month after the incident.

title: The SpaceX IPO tells one story. Here is the more important one.
summary: The article discusses the SpaceX IPO, highlighting its significance beyond just the rockets, valuation, and personalities involved. It emphasizes what the IPO signals about the future direction of the space industry.

title: NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions
summary: NASA has awarded nearly $600 million to three companies for four lunar lander missions as part of its lunar base plans. The agency is also considering launching a spare Mars rover.

title: TechnoMile Recognized among Notable Vendors in Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report
summary: TechnoMile has been recognized as a notable vendor in Forrester's Contract Lifecycle Management Platforms Landscape Report for Q2 2026. The company's AI solution integrates growth, contracts, compliance, and security workflows.

title: Space Force fields mobile satellite-jamming system
summary: The U.S. Space Force has deployed Meadowlands, a mobile satellite-jamming system, marking a shift toward enhanced electronic warfare capabilities. This move reflects the military's growing focus on countering satellite threats.

title: Matthew Williams Joins the Commercial Space Federation as Senior Advisor, National Security
summary: Matthew Williams has joined the Commercial Space Federation (CSF) as Senior Advisor for National Security. Williams previously served as Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense and Chief of Staff.

title: Ocean-monitoring satellite spots wildfire smoke from space | Space photo of the day for July 1, 2026
summary: A NASA satellite designed for ocean monitoring has unexpectedly detected wildfire smoke from space. This discovery highlights an additional application for Earth-observing satellites.

title: New NASA space telescope images reveal the universe in stunning red, white and blue for America 250
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released four stunning cosmic images in red, white, and blue to celebrate America's 250th anniversary. The images showcase celestial wonders as part of the July 4 celebration.

title: Sun unleashes powerful X-class solar flare and Earth-bound CME that could spark northern lights for July 4 weekend
summary: The Sun emitted an X1.1-class solar flare, disrupting radio communications and sending a coronal mass ejection (CME) toward Earth. The CME is expected to arrive on July 3, potentially causing northern lights displays over the July 4 weekend.

title: NASA audit puts Boeing's Starliner under an even bigger microscope: When will it fly astronauts again?
summary: A NASA audit highlights the need for additional astronaut flights to the ISS by 2030 and scrutinizes Boeing's Starliner spacecraft, raising concerns about its readiness and delays.

title: 'PROMISE' me the moon? NASA wants to send spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the lunar surface
summary: NASA plans to send a spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon as part of its Artemis Moon Base program. The mission, named PROMISE, is one of the new lunar landing contracts announced.

title: Rocket Lab aborts launch of Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite at last second
summary: Rocket Lab aborted the launch of a Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite for iQPS just seconds before liftoff. The mission, the eighth for Rocket Lab with iQPS, was halted due to an unspecified issue.

title: Blue Origin starts rebuilding launch pad damaged by New Glenn rocket explosion — and it will look very different when it's done
summary: Blue Origin has begun rebuilding its launch pad after it was damaged by the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The new design will feature significant changes compared to the previous version.

title: ‘Just in time for Canada Day’: Spacewalking astronauts repair space station’s huge Canadarm2
summary: Expedition 74 astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir conducted a seven-hour spacewalk to repair the International Space Station's Canadarm2 on June 30, 2026. The repair was completed just before Canada Day.

title: Mars may have once been filled with seas of magma that made the Red Planet habitable
summary: Seismic data from NASA's InSight mission suggests that Mars may have once had vast magma oceans beneath its crust, potentially making the planet habitable. This discovery sheds light on the Red Planet's ancient geological history.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory and the final launch of an Atlas satellite are scheduled this week. Multiple orbital launches are planned globally.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study galactic structures and dark matter.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX completed a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center after completing 25 flights. The exhibit aims to serve an educational mission.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week will see six orbital launches globally, including the first flight of the Pegasus XL rocket. The launch manifest also includes missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese vehicles.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning using a Falcon 9 rocket. This test marks a significant step in the development of SpaceX's reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 robotic arm as the CRS-34 cargo mission departs. The repairs are part of ongoing maintenance following the departure of the SpaceX Dragon spacecraft.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket program, with its second stage arriving at Stennis Space Center as it targets a 2026 debut. The development marks a key step toward the rocket's planned operational launch.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks the resumption of routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using lunar regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize expenses by utilizing locally sourced materials for structural support and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past failures and awarded contracts for future Moon base missions. The agency plans to use updated versions of existing lander designs for monthly lunar landings with NASA payloads.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: CLPS missions are preparing to deliver landers to the Moon as part of NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services program. This initiative aims to support lunar exploration and scientific research.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's business beyond launch services.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration involves SSC Space, marking Firefly's first European launch site.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches its 2030 extension deadline, with the chair of a NASA advisory committee warning that risk margins for regular operations have reached an alarming low.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX has produced thousands of engineers over 24 years, many of whom have gone on to raise significant funding for new ventures. A new report shows these former SpaceX employees have collectively raised $9.2 billion.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. The acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of NASA's efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.

title: C-Band Round 2: FCC Set to Vote on Another C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC is set to vote in July on auctioning 160 MHz of the upper C-band spectrum, following the first C-band clearing auction. This move aims to further allocate spectrum for wireless services.

title: Thales Alenia Space Wins Software-Defined Satellite Order from Es’hailSat
summary: Thales Alenia Space has secured a software-defined satellite contract from Es’hailSat for the Eshail-3/Türksat-Biruni satellite, which will provide high-speed services. This marks a significant deal for both companies in the Middle Eastern satellite market.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership. The company aims to make space-based infrastructure accessible to sovereign markets.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have inaugurated the first line-fit activation of NSG Skywaves in-flight connectivity (IFC) aboard Saudia’s Airbus A321XLR aircraft. The system is powered by NSG Skywaves and utilizes SES satellite technology.

title: SiriusXM Continues Refresh With SXM-11 Launch
summary: SpaceX launched the SXM-11 satellite for SiriusXM, which will be the highest power satellite in SiriusXM’s fleet. This mission continues SiriusXM's satellite refresh efforts.

title: Rocket Lab’s End-to-End Vision Comes Into Focus With ‘Transformative’ Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab announced a transformative deal to acquire Iridium Communications, advancing its goal of becoming an end-to-end space company. This move integrates launch, manufacturing, and satellite services under one organization.

title: Omar Qaise Looks to Build OQ Technology Into a Sovereign European Pioneer of D2D
summary: OQ Technology, led by CEO and founder Omar Qaise, aims to establish itself as a sovereign European leader in direct-to-device (D2D) technology. The company seeks to become a trusted partner for European governments in the growing D2D market.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted that while commercial markets are prepared for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, there remains a significant gap in effectively communicating these solutions to potential customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD). The deal aims to expand Calian's satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions.
